title: Mistake: GBM for Cross-Subject HAR
summary: **What happened:** Trained LightGBM + XGBoost on 103 handcrafted time/frequency domain features from accelerometer data. OOF F1 was 0.59. LB F1 was 0.24.
what_happened: Trained LightGBM + XGBoost on 103 handcrafted time/frequency domain features from accelerometer data. OOF F1 was 0.59. LB F1 was 0.24.
why_wrong: Statistical features (mean, std, FFT magnitudes, zero-crossing rate) capture how a specific person moves, not what activity is being performed. Each person has a unique movement signature — stride frequency, acceleration amplitude, motion smoothness. GBM memorizes these training-subject patterns and fails on unseen subjects.
general_rule: For cross-subject/cross-domain problems, prefer end-to-end learned features over handcrafted statistical features. GBM + handcrafted features is the default Kaggle approach for tabular data, but time series with domain shift is not truly tabular.
detection_heuristic: If your GBM OOF is much higher than your neural net OOF, but the neural net scores higher on LB — your handcrafted features are overfitting to the training distribution.
